title: Metered Dose Inhaler (MDI with Spacer)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Administration of bronchodilators for mild to moderate bronchospasm.

#### Procedure

- Hold MDI upright and shake well.

- Insert MDI upright into spacer. 
- Note: Aerochamber spaces do not need to be primed.

- Place spacer mouthpiece at patient's lips and get the patient to close their lips to form a good seal. If patient unable to create adequate seal attach an appropriately sized resuscitation mask to the spacer.

- Have the patient breathe out (gently if possible).

- Activate puffer once only. Use the single or four breath technique below. 
- **One (deep breath technique) – most common for adults in daily use:** Have the patient breathe in - slowly and deeply if possible. Instruct the patient to hold their breath for 5 seconds (or as long as they are able if 5 seconds is unachievable). 
- **Four breath technique – used by children or breathless adults:** Have the patient breathe in and out slowly four times.
- **Spacer with face mask:** Place mask over face and nose. After exhalation activate MDI and breathe in and out four times. If using with a child, wait for the child to exhale then activate MDI and coach the child through four breaths.

- Repeat steps until full dose is given.

- Shake the MDI well between each dose.

#### Contraindications

- Nil when indicated.

#### Specific Notes

- The use of an MDI decreases the dissemination of viral particles into the environment surrounding a patient thus decreasing the risk of infection transmission.
- Ensure only one puff at a time when administering. More than one puff causes the medication to stick together and drop to the bottom of the spacer limiting inhalation.

Source: Asthma Australia
